zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Raleigh於1268年出生在英國英格蘭薩默塞特內特萊可姆時，她的父親Warin de Raleigh14歲，母親Joanna le Boteler15歲。她於大約1272年與Andrew de Lutterell在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於1318年2月24日在英國薩默塞特阿生頓享年50歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Raleigh于1268年出生在英国英格兰薩默塞特內特萊可姆时，她的父亲Warin de Raleigh14岁，母亲Joanna le Boteler15岁。她于大约1272年与Andrew de Lutterell在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。她去世于1318年2月24日在英国薩默塞特阿生頓享年50岁。

nameMeanings.name: Raleigh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Raleigh (House) in Pilton (Devon), recorded as Radeleia in 1086 and Rawlegh in 1535. The placename probably derives from Old English rǣge ‘roe deer’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

History: The English explorer Sir Walter Raleigh (1554–1618) was born in Hayes Barton, Devon, into a family of Devon gentry. He was related to most of the West Country's important families, including that of Sir Francis Drake. His half-brother was the explorer Sir Humphrey Gilbert. In 1578 Raleigh was granted a patent to explore and colonize “unknown lands” in America. In the 1580s he was granted extensive estates in Munster (Ireland), in reward for his suppression of the Desmond Rebellions.

variants.name: Rawleigh
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Orlo.
English: variant of   Raleigh  , reflecting a common pronunciation.

variants.name: Riley
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): habitational name chiefly from High Ryley in Accrington (Lancashire) and Riley in Kirkburton, which was in the manor of Wakefield (Yorkshire). In some cases the name may derive from Riley Farm in Eyam (Derbyshire), Royley in Royton (Lancashire), or Rylah in Scarcliffe (Derbyshire). The placenames all come from Old English rȳge ‘rye’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

Irish: from Ó Raghailligh ‘descendant of Raghailleach’, Old Irish Roghallach. This is the name of a chieftain family in Cavan, related to the O'Rourkes. In Anglicized form it has also been confused with Ó Raithile found in Munster, usually Anglicized as O'Rahilly or Rahilly . See O'Reilly .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Raleigh was born in 1268, in Nettlecombe, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, her father, Warin de Raleigh, was 14 and her mother, Joanna le Boteler, was 15. She married Andrew de Lutterell about 1272, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died on 24 February 1318, in Ashington, Somerset, England, at the age of 50.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Raleigh est née en 1268, à Nettlecombe, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Warin de Raleigh, avait 14 ans et sa mère, Joanna le Boteler, avait 15 ans. Elle a épousé Andrew de Lutterell environ 1272, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée le 24 février 1318, à Ashington, Somerset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 50 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Raleigh 1268 in Nettlecombe, Somerset, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Warin de Raleigh 14 und ihre Mutter Joanna le Boteler 15. Sie hat um 1272 in England, United Kingdom Andrew de Lutterell geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Sie starb am 24. Februar 1318 im Alter von 50 Jahren in Ashington, Somerset, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Raleigh è nata il nel 1268 nel Nettlecombe, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Warin de Raleigh, aveva 14 anni e sua madre, Joanna le Boteler, aveva 15 anni. Lei sposato Andrew de Lutterell circa 1272, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì il 24 febbraio 1318, nel Ashington, Somerset, England, all'età di 50 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Raleigh(이)가 1268년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 네틀렉브에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Warin de Raleigh(은)는 14세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joanna le Boteler(은)는 15세였다. 그녀는 대략 1272년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Andrew de Lutterell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1318년 2월 24일 영국 서머세트 애신그턴에서 50세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Raleigh родилась в 1268 г. в местности неттлекомб, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Warin de Raleigh было 14 лет, а матери, которую звали Joanna le Boteler, 15. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Andrew de Lutterell около 1272 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она умерла 24 февраля 1318 г. в местности ашингтон, сомирсет, Англия в возрасте 50 лет.
